description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: census-designated place in Pulaski County, Missouri, United States
description_wiki: Fort Leonard Wood is a U.S. Army training installation located in the Missouri Ozarks.

description: Semi-automatic rifle
description_wiki: The M1 Garand or M1 rifle is a semi-automatic rifle that was the service rifle of the U.S. Army during World War II and the Korean War.

description: neighborhood in Chicago, Illinois, USA
description_wiki: Humboldt Park is one of the 77 community areas of Chicago in Illinois, United States, located on the West Side of Chicago.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

surface_forms: Fort Bliss, Texas, at El Paso
description: census designated place in Texas, United States
description_wiki: Fort Bliss is a United States Army post in New Mexico and Texas, with its headquarters in El Paso, Texas.

description: U.S. Army facility located adjacent to Aberdeen, Maryland
description_wiki: Aberdeen Proving Ground (APG) is a U.S. Army facility located adjacent to Aberdeen, Maryland, United States.

surface_forms: Vietnam, one was killed in Vietnam
description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: defunct airline in the United States (1930–2001)
description_wiki: Trans World Airlines (TWA) was a trunk carrier, a scheduled airline in the United States that operated from 1930 until it was acquired by American Airlines in 2001.

surface_forms: Travis Airbase in San Francisco, California
description: US Air Force base near Fairfield, California, United States
description_wiki: Travis Air Force Base (IATA: SUU, ICAO: KSUU, FAA LID: SUU) is a United States Air Force base under the operational control of Air Mobility Command (AMC), located three miles (5 km) east of the central business district of the city of Fairfield, in Solano County, California.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: city in and county seat of Honolulu County, Hawaii, United States, that is also the capital city of Hawaii
description_wiki: Honolulu (  HON-ə-LOO-loo; Hawaiian: [honoˈlulu]) is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Hawaii, located in the Pacific Ocean.

description: United States Air Force base in Japan, former Japanese WW2 airfield
description_wiki: Kadena Air Base (嘉手納飛行場, Kadena Hikōjō) (IATA: DNA, ICAO: RODN) is a United States Air Force base in the towns of Kadena and Chatan and the city of Okinawa, in Okinawa Prefecture, Japan.

description: military airfield in Vietnam
description_wiki: Bien Hoa Air Base (Vietnamese: Sân bay Biên Hòa) is a Vietnam Air Defence - Air Force (VN ADAF) military airfield located in South-Central southern Vietnam about 25 km (16 miles) from Ho Chi Minh City,  across the Dong Nai river in the northern ward of Tân Phong, and within the city of Biên Hòa within Đồng Nai Province.

description: 1927–2025 airline in the United States, former primary international carrier
description_wiki: Pan American World Airways, originally founded as Pan American Airways and more commonly known as Pan Am, was an airline that was the principal and largest international air carrier and unofficial flag carrier of the United States for much of the 20th century.

description: International airport in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam
description_wiki: Tan Son Nhat International Airport (IATA: SGN, ICAO: VVTS) is an international airport serving Ho Chi Minh City, the most populous city in Vietnam.

description: US military base at Long Bình ward, 1965–1975
description_wiki: Long Binh Post (Tổng kho Long Bình) is a former U.S. Army base located in Long Bình, Đồng Nai between Biên Hòa and Saigon, Vietnam.

description: military unit
active United States Army formation
description_wiki: The 4th Infantry Division is a division of the United States Army based at Fort Carson, Colorado.

description: active United States Army formation
description_wiki: The 25th Infantry Division (nicknamed "Tropic Lightning") is a United States Army division based at Schofield Barracks in Hawaii.

description: network of underground tunnels used by Viet Cong soldiers during combat
description_wiki: The tunnels of Củ Chi (Vietnamese: Địa đạo Củ Chi) are an immense network of connecting tunnels located in the Củ Chi District of Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon), Vietnam, and are part of a much larger network of tunnels that underlie much of the country.

description: active United States Army formation, oldest continuously serving division in the Regular Army
description_wiki: The 1st Infantry Division (1ID) is a combined arms division of the United States Army, and is the oldest continuously serving division in the Regular Army.

description: military base in southern Vietnam
largest city in Vietnam
description_wiki: Ho Chi Minh City (HCMC; Vietnamese: Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, IPA: [tʰaɲ˨˩ fow˦˥ ho˨˩ cɪj˦˥ mɨn˧˧] ), also known as Saigon, is the most populous municipality of Vietnam, with a population of more than 14 million in 2025.

description: province of Vietnam in Southern Vietnam
armored recovery vehicle
description_wiki: The M88 recovery vehicle is one of the largest armored recovery vehicles (ARV) in use by United States Armed Forces.

description: 1968 military campaign during the Vietnam War
description_wiki: The Tet Offensive was both a major escalation and one of the largest military campaigns of the Vietnam War.

description: sole company of TBS Class 1-68 with 219 graduates
company within the United States Army
Part of the Vietnam War (1967)
description_wiki: Operation Kole Kole was a search and destroy operation during the Vietnam War conducted by the 2nd Brigade, 25th Infantry Division that took place in Hậu Nghĩa and Tây Ninh Provinces, lasting from 14 May to 7 December 1967.

description: unincorporated community in Honolulu County, Hawaii, United States of America
